subject: Chemistry
chapter: The d and f Block Elements

remember: Students will be able to recall the general electronic configuration of d-block and f-block elements.
Students will be able to remember the characteristics of d-block and f-block elements.
Students will be able to identify the position of d-block and f-block elements in the periodic table.
understand: Students will be able to explain the electronic configuration of d-block and f-block elements in terms of orbitals.
Students will be able to understand the relationship between the electronic configuration and the properties of d-block and f-block elements.
Students will be able to explain how the electronic configuration affects the chemical properties of d-block and f-block elements.
apply: Students will be able to apply the electronic configuration to predict the chemical properties of d-block and f-block elements.
Students will be able to use the electronic configuration to explain the trends in the properties of d-block and f-block elements.
Students will be able to apply the knowledge of d-block and f-block elements to solve problems related to chemical reactions and synthesis.
analyze: Students will be able to analyze the relationship between the electronic configuration and the properties of d-block and f-block elements.
Students will be able to compare and contrast the properties of d-block and f-block elements with those of s-block elements.
Students will be able to evaluate the importance of d-block and f-block elements in various industrial and biological processes.

ncert_objectives: To explain the electronic configuration of d-block and f-block elements.
To describe the characteristics of d-block and f-block elements.
To explain the relationship between the electronic configuration and the properties of d-block and f-block elements.
board_exam_objectives: To identify the position of d-block and f-block elements in the periodic table.
To explain the trends in the properties of d-block and f-block elements.
To apply the knowledge of d-block and f-block elements to solve problems related to chemical reactions and synthesis.
